description: This file defines negative yes–no question formation in the Past Perfect tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). Negative questions are formed by adding the sentence-final negation marker 'gba' followed by the question marker 'we?'.

general_principle.statement: Past perfect negative questions in Balanda retain the past perfect prefix 'taa-' and form negation with sentence-final 'gba', followed by the question marker 'we?'.

constraints: The prefix 'taa-' must remain attached to the verb
Word order remains identical to affirmative clauses

preposition_behavior.rule: All prepositions retain their original form and position in negative questions.

error_constraints: Do not place 'gba' before the verb

balanda: Ne taateli gbi famvara gba we?
english: Had I not slept in the bed?

balanda: Ne taanonö gbi difo gba we?
english: Had I not walked in the compound?

balanda: Uku taamande gbi njü gba we?
english: Had Uku not worked in the garden?

balanda: Ne taamoliki gbi difo gba we?
english: Had I not played in the compound?
Had I not watched the cat?
Had I not read a book?
Had I not thought about it?

balanda: Ne taavele zi ndi gba we?
Ne taamaguku ti dha gba we?

english: Had I not tied the shoe?
Had I not cut the pot?
Had I not pulled the door?
Had I not pushed the box?
Had I not washed the shoe?
Had I not hidden the beads?
Had I not built a house?
Had I not changed the words?
Had I not turned the door?

balanda: Ne taatoko ti vili gba we?

english: Had I not initiated the project?

balanda: Ne taamaforo ti mira gba we?
english: Had I not contributed to the project?
Had I not separated the trash?
Had I not upheld the law?
Had I not tried the cloth?
Had I not sharpened the knife?
Had I not started the project?
